title: Accessibility - Large Text
scenario: Poly Studio Mobile app screens display truncated text or layout issues when Android Large Text accessibility setting is set to maximum
steps: 1. On the Android device, go to Settings → Accessibility → Text and Display → Font Size (or Large Text).
2. Enable the 'Larger Text' toggle and drag the slider to the maximum size setting.
3. Launch the Poly Studio Mobile app.
4. Observe the Home screen for any text truncation, overlapping, or layout breakage.
5. Navigate to Device Settings by tapping on a connected Poly device.
6. Observe all text elements in Device Settings for proper enlargement and no truncation.
7. Navigate to Audio Settings → ANC, Transparency Mode, and Equalizer screens.
8. Observe each screen for text display and layout integrity.
9. Navigate to the DFU (Device Firmware Update) screen if available.
10. Observe the DFU screen for proper text display.
11. Navigate through any Onboarding or Quick Guide screens (e.g., first-launch walkthroughs).
12. Observe all onboarding/guide screens for enlarged text without layout issues.
13. Navigate to the Notifications and Account screens.
14. Observe these screens for proper text rendering and layout.
expected: All app screens (Home, Device Settings, Audio Settings, ANC, Transparency Mode, Equalizer, DFU, Onboarding/Quick Guides, Notifications, Account) display enlarged text properly without truncation, overlapping, or layout breakage when Android's Large Text accessibility setting is enabled at maximum size.
